assertions.text: Output contains at least one markdown table
assertions.evidence:
| Scenario | Current Status | Requested Status | Allowed? | Reason? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standard order confirmation | PENDING | CONFIRMED | yes | |

assertions.text: Table covers cancellation rules — includes rows for states where cancellation is allowed and where it is not
assertions.evidence: | Customer cancels before confirmation | PENDING | CANCELLED | yes | | ... | Customer cancels after confirmation | CONFIRMED | CANCELLED | yes | | ... | Cancellation attempt after dispatch | SHIPPED | CANCELLED | no | Already dispatched |

assertions.text: The 30-day return window rule is addressed — either as a threshold column, a separate table, or flagged as an open question
assertions.evidence: Table 2: Return Request Eligibility contains a 'Return Window (Policy)' column showing '30 days' for all rows, and rows such as '| Return on last day | DELIVERED | 30 | 30 days | yes |' and '| Return one day late | DELIVERED | 31 | 30 days | no | Return window expired |'

assertions.text: Uses value sets {PENDING, CONFIRMED} or equivalent to express 'regardless of which starting state' for cases where a rule holds across states
assertions.evidence: | Return attempted on non-delivered order | {PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED} | | 30 days | no | Order not yet delivered |

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Column headers include 'Allowed?' and 'Return Eligible?' and 'Reason?' — e.g., '| Scenario | Current Status | Requested Status | Allowed? | Reason? |'
